mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust domestic demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive rank correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple market cycles, or positive returns occur in < 45% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces demand destruction, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during tightening periods, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates restrictive real rates that suppress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The real rate spread shows no predictive power for 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or positive returns dominate in > 60% of high real rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively or neutrally with 60-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, with a Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real personal consumption expenditures exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp contraction in consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in > 70% of instances following a > 0.5% RSAFS decline, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below 50% out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates lingering supply chain constraints that limit corporate output and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recovery phases are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 65% of cases, or the mechanism yields a negative information coefficient over a 2-year walk-forward test.
